claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have one child - Alex, who is 14 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Alex.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I have a few more questions:

Are you responsible for Alex? This typically means the child lives with you, or you contribute to the cost of looking after them at least the same amount as Child Benefit would provide.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me and I'm responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about your own situation. Are you aged 16 or over?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. 

A couple more questions about Alex:

Is Alex in full-time education or training, or has Alex left education/training? At 14, I'd expect Alex is still in school, but I just want to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex is still in school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to ask about your residency status. Do you live in the UK? And are you either:
- A British or Irish citizen, or
- A person with permission to access public funds (such as settled status, or certain visa types)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know.

Since you're living abroad, I have a few follow-up questions to understand your situation better:

Are you working for the UK government or serving in the UK armed forces while living abroad? Or is there any other connection to the UK, such as receiving certain UK benefits or being employed by a UK-based company?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't work for the UK government or armed forces. I don't have any of those connections to the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me check the specific eligibility rules regarding residency to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I now have enough information to make a decision about your eligibility for Child Benefit.

The rules state that you "normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**." While there are some exceptions for people living abroad (such as Crown servants working for the UK government or those going to certain countries), you've confirmed that you don't work for the UK government, armed forces, or have other qualifying connections to the UK.

Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, it appears you would not be eligible for Child Benefit because the residency requirement is not met.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on my assessment, I'm sorry to say that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

**Summary:**

| Child | Eligible? |
|-------|-----------|
| Alex | No |

**Reason:**
To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. While Alex meets all the requirements for being a qualifying child (under 16 and in full-time education, living with you), the key issue is that you are currently living abroad.

There are some exceptions for people living outside the UK, such as Crown servants (those working for the UK government abroad) or members of the armed forces, but you've confirmed these don't apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you return to live in the UK—you may then be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. I hope this information is helpful, and I'm sorry I couldn't give you better news today.

reasonings: To qualify for Child Benefit, the claimant normally needs to: (1) be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education/training), and (2) live in the UK. While Alex meets the child eligibility criteria (aged 14 and in full-time education, living with the claimant who is responsible for them), the claimant does not meet the residency requirement. The claimant confirmed they live abroad and do not work for the UK government, serve in the UK armed forces, or have any other qualifying connection to the UK that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not live in the UK and do not meet any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim (such as being a Crown servant or having other qualifying UK connections). While the child Alex meets all the requirements for a qualifying child, the claimant themselves do not meet the residency requirements for receiving Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: To qualify for Child Benefit, the claimant normally needs to: (1) be responsible for a child under 16 (or under 20 if in approved education/training), and (2) live in the UK. While Alex meets the child eligibility criteria (aged 14 and in full-time education, living with the claimant who is responsible for them), the claimant does not meet the residency requirement. The claimant confirmed they live abroad and do not work for the UK government, serve in the UK armed forces, or have any other qualifying connection to the UK that would allow them to claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not live in the UK and do not meet any of the exceptions that would allow someone living abroad to claim (such as being a Crown servant or having other qualifying UK connections). While the child Alex meets all the requirements for a qualifying child, the claimant themselves do not meet the residency requirements for receiving Child Benefit.
